title: Tool Choice, Evidence, Actions, and Approval Boundaries · Prompt Lens: Approval Boundary
summary: Design a tool-using investigation that reads evidence before changing state. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt approval boundary increment in a safe tool-use decision protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Require approval for consequential, external, destructive, or authority-expanding actions.
Base the next decision on actual tool results and distinguish inference from observation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.
